# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React 18 and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with a clear separation of concerns.

### Core Technologies
- **React 18**: Modern React with concurrent features
- **TypeScript**: Type-safe development
- **Vite**: Fast build tool and development server
- **React Router DOM**: Client-side routing
- **Supabase**: Backend-as-a-Service providing database, authentication, and real-time features
- **React Query**: Server state management and caching
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first CSS framework
- **shadcn/ui**: High-quality React components built on Radix UI

## Application Structure

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders the root App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing and providers

### Key Components
- **AppLayout**: Main layout wrapper with sidebar and topbar
- **AppSidebar**: Collapsible navigation sidebar with organized menu sections
- **TopBar**: Header displaying current page title
- **BeudoxLogo**: Custom logo component with variant support
- **NavLink**: Wrapper around React Router's NavLink for consistent styling

### UI Component Library
The application uses shadcn/ui components extensively, including:
- Form components (input, select, checkbox, etc.)
- Layout components (accordion, tabs, dialog, etc.)
- Data display (table, chart, etc.)
- Feedback components (toast, alert, etc.)

### Routing & Navigation
- BrowserRouter for client-side routing
- Protected routes with authentication checks
- Organized navigation sections: MAIN, PEOPLE, FINANCE, WORK, SYSTEM
- Dynamic page titles based on current route

### Authentication Flow
- Supabase-based authentication
- AuthProvider context for session management
- ProtectedRoute component wrapping authenticated pages
- Automatic redirects based on authentication state

### State Management
- React Query for server state (API calls, caching)
- React context for authentication state
- Local component state for UI interactions

## Database & Backend

### Supabase Integration
- PostgreSQL database via Supabase
- Row Level Security (RLS) policies
- Real-time subscriptions for live updates
- Authentication with JWT tokens
- File storage capabilities

### Key Database Concepts
- Employee management with profiles and roles
- Attendance and time tracking
- Leave management system
- Payroll and financial data
- Project and evaluation tracking

## Development Workflow

### Build & Development
- Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- TypeScript for type checking
- ESLint for code quality
- Vitest for unit testing
- Playwright for end-to-end testing

### Styling Approach
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- CSS custom properties for design tokens
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach
- Dark/light theme support via next-themes

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for performant form management
- Zod for schema validation
- @hookform/resolvers for integration

## Key Files & Directories

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

### Core Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication hook
- `src/hooks/use-toast.ts`: Toast notification system
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Supabase client and types

### Pages
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page (redirects based on auth)
- `src/pages/Login.tsx`: Authentication page
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`: Main dashboard
- Additional pages for employees, attendance, etc. (routes partially implemented)

## Business Logic Patterns

### Data Fetching
- React Query for declarative data fetching
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Error handling with user-friendly messages
- Loading states with skeleton components

### User Experience
- Responsive design for all screen sizes
- Accessible components using Radix UI primitives
- Toast notifications for feedback
- Loading spinners during async operations

### Security
- Authentication required for protected routes
- Supabase RLS for data access control
- Environment variables for sensitive configuration
- Type-safe API interactions

## Development Notes

- The application is currently in early development with core routing and authentication implemented
- Many feature pages (employees, attendance, etc.) have navigation links but may not have full implementations yet
- The sidebar navigation structure indicates planned features for comprehensive HR management
- Supabase migrations exist, suggesting database schema is being developed
- Testing setup is configured but test coverage may be limited in early stages
